name: NASDAQ, INC.
summary: Nasdaq, Inc. operates global exchanges and provides financial technology solutions. It operates The Nasdaq Stock Market, multiple U.S. options exchanges (Nasdaq PHLX, Nasdaq ISE, Nasdaq GEMX, Nasdaq MRX, Nasdaq BX Options, The Nasdaq Options Market), and Nordic/Baltic exchanges under Nasdaq Nordic and Nasdaq Baltic brands. It also offers clearing via Nasdaq Clearing AB, and technology/data services including SaaS solutions through its Adenza acquisition, serving capital markets participants worldwide.

products.name: The Nasdaq Stock Market
products.description: U.S. cash equity exchange and primary listing venue for equities including ETPs.

products.name: Nasdaq PHLX
products.description: U.S. options exchange and cash equity exchange (Nasdaq PSX) operated by Nasdaq PHLX LLC.

products.name: Nasdaq ISE
products.description: U.S. options exchange operated by Nasdaq ISE, LLC.

products.name: Nasdaq GEMX
products.description: U.S. options exchange operated by Nasdaq GEMX, LLC.

products.name: Nasdaq MRX
products.description: U.S. options exchange operated by Nasdaq MRX, LLC.

products.name: Nasdaq BX / Nasdaq BX Options
products.description: U.S. cash equity exchange and options exchange operated by Nasdaq BX, Inc.

products.name: The Nasdaq Options Market
products.description: U.S. options exchange operated by The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC.

products.name: Nasdaq Nordic
products.description: Group of Nordic exchanges and clearing including Stockholm, Copenhagen, Helsinki, Iceland, and Nasdaq Clearing AB.

products.name: Nasdaq Baltic
products.description: Group of Baltic exchanges including Tallinn, Riga, and Vilnius.

products.name: Nasdaq First North
products.description: Alternative marketplaces for smaller and growth companies in Nordic and Baltic regions.

products.name: Nasdaq Clearing
products.description: Central counterparty clearing operations conducted by Nasdaq Clearing AB.

products.name: Adenza
products.description: Financial technology software solutions acquired by Nasdaq, offered as SaaS to capital markets clients.

products.name: CAT (Consolidated Audit Trail)
products.description: Market-wide consolidated audit trail established under SEC-approved plan by Nasdaq and other exchanges.

partnerships.description: AWS referenced as a technology partner; mentioned in abbreviations list context of Nasdaq's technology infrastructure.
